programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams find items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp also visits the Stamford Mercury News Archive(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Things are difficult for Ruhma and Doug when Carrie is taken to hospital. Rob and PC Dajani try to work out who is telling the truth between two sisters in a complicated arson case(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: The shooting of two men links a slick casino owner to a suffering woman and her rash son. DI Bradford gets some bad news. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small and Bailey Patrick(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a drop-arm sofa, an old chest and a train set from Witley Recycling Centre, enlisting the help of restorer Peter Fournel and upcycler Jacqui Joseph(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel around Yorkshire, finding such rarities as a vintage set of weights and a Victorian skirt lifter, before an auction in Rotherham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Alex Scott host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, where the panel discuss the coronavirus and the implications that are affecting the UK and the rest of the world(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: The McGregors receives devastating news that rocks their family, while Kamal is inaugurated as prime minister and tightens racial laws across Albion - all the while battling to keep his personal secrets in the past. Sephy desperately tries to get through to Callum, but he is being pulled further and further into the Liberation Militia. Jude wants his brother to listen to Dorn - he is the one who is making a difference. As Callum takes matters into his own hands, Meggie introduces the Hadleys to someone who makes Sephy question everything she has ever known(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Danny and Steve 'welcome' new boss Ted Case into the UCOS office in Sasha's absence, but for all his experience, keen eye for detail and a killer instinct for detecting liars, they aren't as sold on his superstitious quirks. While trying to work one another out, the team treads carefully in the case of a murdered vicar who received racist hate mail in the lead up to his death. With emotions running high, it's up to Ted to pull together his new unit and keep the peace in one of UCOS's most sensitive cases. Starring Larry Lamb, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: The shooting of two men links a slick casino owner to a suffering woman and her rash son. DI Bradford gets some bad news. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small and Bailey Patrick(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help two teams find items at Detling Antiques Fair in Kent, while Natasha Raskin Sharp views a family's impressive collection of novelty teapots(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. The team set to work repairing a Jamaican pump organ, an RAF bomber pilot's hat, a naval rigging tool kit, and a crib made from wood salvaged from a church that was bombed during the First World War(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores nature at its most perilous on the wild waters of Britain's coast. Nick Crane reveals why the first lifeboat station was founded on the north-east coast at Bamburgh Castle, Mark Horton finds out how ferries cope with stormy seas, and Miranda Krestovnikoff explores the jellyfish of Dublin Bay - and how both swimmers and scientists are seeking an antidote to its excruciating sting. Dick Strawbridge investigates one of the strangest stories of the Second World War - did the Germans really invade the south coast in 1940?(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeals for funds on behalf of a registered charity(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Kate Bliss takes on Caroline Hawley at an auction in Kent and veteran Eric Knowles challenges rookie Chuko Ojiri at a car boot sale in Chesterfield(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Bridget and Vincent are pawns in an imminent atrocity. The accomplice in Donna's disposal is exposed(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The troubleshooter tackles Amelies, a harbour-side seafood restaurant in the picturesque Cornish town of Porthleven. Owner Sam Sheffield Dunstan gave up a high-flying career in London five years ago to fulfil a lifetime ambition of opening an eatery. However, her dream turned into a nightmare as she failed to attract the right head chef, so a desperate Sam turned to Alex to help find the best person for the job. In this edition, Alex puts the first candidates through their paces(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts from Rochester Cathedral in Kent, where antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot falls for a book of fairy tales illustrated by Arthur Rackham, and Adam Partridge discovers an elegant chiming Swiss pocket watch. Paul also meets one of the last remaining survivors of the Dunkirk evacuation during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Susie Dent, Nick Owen, Chemmy Alcott and Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills in the quiz, all hoping they stand a chance of winning Friday's final(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle follow a male lion being introduced to eight females in the hope of starting a whole new pride in the park. Plus, how elephants keep maintain the strength of their trunks in captivity and an attempt to mate two emus(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams venture through Peru's high altitudes as the race reaches the halfway mark - but the first hurdle they face is bypassing the ongoing state of emergency in Ecuador, and drastic measures are required to ensure the competitors' safety. Emon and Jamiul are in the lead but massively over budget, so try to get work with some local fishermen, while Dom and Lizzie discover a connection to their grandmother in Peru's capital(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns reflect on the lessons learned through the series, and how corner shops can continue to thrive in the changing world of 21st-century business. The programme examines how shops have reinvented themselves to compete with mini-supermarkets, the rise of zero-waste shops, and how other businesses, like cafes, coffee shops and micro pubs, are filling the role of community hub(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: The Staffordshire Police officers look to the future as they take on new recruits, including trainee Holly Swift. After working in mental health services and seeing the difference police officers can make, she decided it was the career for her. At the other end of the career path is Sgt Dave Allan - who is just about to complete 30 years' service and retire. The police service recruited heavily after the miners' strikes in the 1980s, and many of those officers are now approaching retirement, including Dave(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Rhys James and Rosie Jones and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Sorry
programme.desc: Comedy short, written by and starring Lolly Adefope. An actress who considers herself on the verge of superstardom heads to the London premiere of her first Hollywood film. However, during the journey, she finds herself at the centre of a scandal on the internet that could destroy her career(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary looks at Alder Hey Children's Hospital's craniofacial department - one of only four in the UK that treats life-threatening congenital skull and facial abnormalities. With a rise in referrals, the department is over-subscribed and neurosurgeon Chris Parks, who leads the craniofacial surgical team, faces a non-stop workload. Chris always works hand in glove with Ben Robertson, one of three paediatric maxillofacial surgeons in the UK. But this partnership is about to be broken up, as Ben is leaving to return to his native Australia. It takes 23 years' training to become a maxillofacial Consultant specialising in cranio-surgery, and there is currently nobody experienced enough to replace Ben, putting extra pressure on the waiting lists(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A colourful hair entrepreneur offers one Dragon a new look - and all of them a new investment opportunity. A cheesecake-maker has sweet dreams of a cash boost for his franchise model, but will his pitch be the stuff of nightmares? A husband-and-wife team hope their electrical invention will make sparks fly in the Den, as rival Dragons Deborah Meaden and Sara Davies line up to try it out. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as six more hopefuls compete in the third week of heats. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for former contestants who have gone on to work in the food industry(n)

programme.title: Child of Our Time
programme.desc: In 1999, the groundbreaking BBC series Child of Our Time began filming a group of babies from the moment they were born, to explore what would shape their lives in the new millennium. Twenty years on ,this programme catches us with Eve, Jamie and Rhianna from the project, with the three reflecting on growing up during a time of extraordinary social change(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram assess items brought to the Old College in Aberystwyth, while presenter Paul Martin visits a Regency restoration project at the National Botanic Garden of Wales(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian learn about the new building built for Exeter Deaf Academy. Plus, Vilma Jackson tries on Cute Circuit's Sound Shirt. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane follows the Welsh coast path around Anglesey, presenting tales of inspired thinking and coastal ingenuity. Tess Dunlop reveals how a small French fishing village gave rise to the birth of British commercial radio in the 1930s, Brendan Walker investigates why the 1970 collapse of Pembrokeshire's Cleddau Bridge changed the engineering world for ever, Dick Strawbridge is in Portsmouth, where he tells of a novel First World War plan to disguise British ships by hiding them in plain sight(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian takes on Eric Knowles at a car boot sale in Essex, where questionable bric-a-brac and bad weather are the order of the day(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian face off at an auction in Somerset, where Eric spots a sleeper in a job lot of china(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Stephen K Amos joins Brian Conley to look back at the TV shows that shaped him, and led him to become a comedian. From the singing pigs Pinky and Perky, to the terrifying Tales of the Unexpected by way of entertainer Danny la Rue, Stephen's early small-screen influences are wide and varied - but why does he believe he owes his entire career to TV adverts for vacuum cleaners?(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi puts three more head chef candidates through their paces as they compete for a vacancy at Amelies, a seafood restaurant in Porthleven, Cornwall. With one candidate already through to the final, the next three applicants must endure a gruelling interview process. Amiable Irishman Barry hopes to impress with a main course of hare and rainbow pasta, while 20-year-old Chris wonders whether his decision to put a duo of lamb on a seafood menu might prove to be his undoing. Plus, experienced chef Paul refuses to let his work speak for itself as he tries to convince owner Sam he is the right man for the job(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire, Catherine Southon enthuses about a diamond ring, and Charles Hanson enjoys trying on a pair of signed boxing gloves(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes as kidney disease claims the life of one of the park's Southern Australian koalas. A little bird with a big personality proves difficult to catch for the keepers(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Sardinia, starting out in Cagliari, where everyone is in high spirits for the feast of St Efisio. The bikers join in by cooking a local speciality, almond and orange cakes. They also meet the tuna fishermen of Carloforte and a chef who has a special way of serving up platters of the fish, are shown how to make sheep's black pudding by a group of shepherds and are served a stew by the 'King of Sardinia'(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Gordon Jones, Roberta Hall, Ross Bryans and Amy Elles compete in the heats for Scotland. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including a venison tartare with smoked celeriac remoulade which pays homage to J K Rowling's Harry Potter franchise, and a Wind in the Willows inspired dish featuring a sourdough baguette and chicken terrine(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part two of two. The conclusion of the documentary follows three gifted teenage girls, Anne Marie, Jada and Shakira, as they make decisions about their futures. In Port Talbot, Anne Marie is more settled after moving in with her best friend, but isolation from her family and nerves about her exams are starting to trouble her. Meanwhile, would-be paediatrician Jada has her heart set on taking her A Levels at grammar school, and Tamworth-based Shakira has got to make up lost ground if she is to achieve the high grades her teachers have predicted(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Comedy following the misadventures of Leslie Winner, an eternally-optimistic yet calamitous character, as he prepares for married life. When one of his painters call in sick, Leslie's father-in-law Chris reluctantly agrees to let him take on a decorating job for an elderly couple. Starring Spencer Jones and Shaun Williamson(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that has - and hasn't - happened in the news this week(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider the profit-making potential of more business ideas. A German-Portuguese partnership proposes a new kind of camera lens for mobile phones, a lifelong baker shares her recipe for vegan treats, and a third team pitches their idea for intravenous vitamins. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian learn about the new building built for Exeter Deaf Academy. Plus, Vilma Jackson tries on Cute Circuit's Sound Shirt. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams head out of Copan Ruinas in Western Honduras to their next checkpoint - Panama City. The journey sees them travel across vast swathes of Central America, passing through El Salvador, Nicaragua, Costa Rica and finally Panama. Mother and son Jo and Sam are the first to set off, and having raced hard in the previous leg, they're keen to experience more this time round. Working at a hostel in El Salvador offers some respite from the race, and gives the pair the chance to help with a turtle conservation project(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on medically fit patients stranded in hospital due to a lack of after-care. They include a 70-year-old vascular dementia patient who is stuck in Aintree University Hospital until he and his wife can find a residential home to deal with his complex needs. Plus, the story of a three-year-old with a rare genetic disease who has been waiting in hospital for eight months while his local council take the extraordinary decision to build him and his family an entire house equipped with everything the youngster requires(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Two friends are rescued from shark infested seas, and petrol station workers in Birmingham save a woman from car jackers. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones speaks to Archbishop of York designate the Rt Rev Stephen Cottrell about the Christian hope that can still be found despite the current troubled times. Aled also refelcts on recent editions of the programme in which he met those whose faith saw them through especially challenging times - inlcuding a woman who survived the Grenfell Tower tragedy, and a Second World War veteran inolved in the D-Day landings(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, with Fiona Bruce going aboard the Discovery to reveal the epic story of the ship and Antarctic explorer Robert Falcon Scott. Items brought in to be assessed by the experts include a self-pouring teapot, a glass helmet and a piece of tartan that has been to the Moon(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team investigates the culture, history and legends of Britain and its people, beginning with the nation's islands. Nick Crane learns how Fair Isle's small community has survived and Neil Oliver visits Orkney to explore conspiracy theories surrounding the death of Lord Kitchener. Tessa Dunlop heads for the Western Isles to investigate an optical phenomenon known as the green ray, Andy Torbet climbs a chalk pinnacle on the Isle of Wight, and folk singer June Tabor performs a traditional ballad(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian visit Sint-Truiden in Belgium, where Danny finds an antique sewing machine and Eric finds out how modern bronzes are cast(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Writer and comedy actor Adil Ray joins Brian Conley to discuss the shows of his youth, and to explain how the Liverpool-set Bread inspired him to create Asian sitcom Citizen Khan(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The last three candidates are challenged to cook fish in a wood-fired oven as Alex continues her mission to find a head chef for Amelies seafood restaurant in Cornwall. Portuguese fisherwoman Celia takes a risk by serving up roasted octopus, Frenchman Fabien fails to tell his sea bass from his sea bream and Robert may be cool in the kitchen but struggles to take criticism. With only one place left in the final, the pressure is on(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Michael Baggott assess objects brought into the University of Nottingham, while presenter Paul Martin learns about a Nottingham-born Victorian prize-fighter called Bendigo and meets locals who are keeping his memory alive(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Countdown's Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste continue the daily test of their skills, hosted by Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The vet is called when Violet the koala goes downhill, while Iggy Pop the iguana has a mystery tail injury that requires surgery - but at his age an operation is a risky business(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which adventurer Paul Rose explores the 79 miles of the Yorkshire Wolds Way, one of Britain's least well-known national walking trails. The trail starts at the Humber Estuary and ends at the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Filey. On the way, Paul takes in the views from the top of the Humber Bridge, learns to ride a penny farthing and searches out the spots made internationally famous by the artist David Hockney(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of the Lancashire village Sunderland Point, which is cut off twice a day by the tide and has become a haven for wildlife. He reveals it was once a busy port that was instrumental in the slave trade, and at the forefront of the luxury goods business that transformed 18th-century Britain.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs are at the halfway point of their heat, serving up their mains and desserts celebrating children's literature. One of the contestants attempts an ambitious nose-to-tail rabbit dish as a homage to Toad's feasting in The Wind in the Willows, while another takes inspiration from Dundee's comic hero Desperate Dan and tries to replicate the cowboy's famous cow pie using Scottish beef fillet, beef cheeks, bone marrow rissole and puff pastry. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The final episode looks at radical new treatments and surgeries offering cancer patients hopes of survival, including two-year-old Yeshua, who has a tumour in his abdomen. In Iraq, where he was born, Yeshua was given a five per cent chance of survival. Though high doses of chemotherapy have shrunk the tumour over the past six months, in order for Yeshua to survive he needs an extremely complicated 12-hour operation at Alder Hey Children's Hospital to remove as much of the growth as possible(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Glaswegian comedian Frankie Boyle introduces young stand-up sensation Jack Carroll, who rose to fame on Britain's Got Talent in 2013. Holly Walsh will also be taking to the stage at London's Hammersmith Apollo(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Return of the documentary following three gifted teenagers, Liam, Kian and Jamarley, as they prepare to finish secondary school and make important decisions about their futures. Kian dreams of becoming a theoretical physicist, but has to face up to the reality of having to leave Hartlepool to pursue his dreams. In Newcastle, aspiring doctor Liam finds his work ethic is increasingly under scrutiny, while in London, Jamarley faces pressures outside school(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain: The Greatest Discoveries
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts re-examines key archaeological sites of prehistoric Britain(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines Gauguin's Vision After the Sermon,an epic 17th-century religious painting exploring temptation and desire. He uncovers some unusual connections with sumo wrestling, Victor Hugo's Les Miserables, and a 17-year-old girl called Madeleine(n)

programme.title: Britain Beneath Your Feet
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary in which Dallas Campbell takes a unique look at Britain, exploring the hidden wonders beneath people's feet. Along the way he finds out how the Shard of London stays standing on soft clay and reveals how Edinburgh was set upon an ancient volcano. The presenter also abseils down an underground waterfall higher than Niagara, canoes along a secret river under the city of Bristol and discovers a vast root system beneath one of the nation's oldest oak trees(n)

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: An investigation into how recent archaeological finds on the archipelago indicate that Orkney was the cultural capital of Stone Age Britain and the birthplace of the stone circle cult. Chris Packham uncovers the secrets revealed by the DNA of Orkney's unique vole, Neil Oliver explores tombs and monuments, Shini Somara experiments to discover how ancient Orcadians could have moved giant blocks of stone over rough ground, and Andy Torbet climbs a challenging sea-stack to unlock the story of Orkney's unusual geology(n)

programme.title: Soup Cans & Superstars: How Pop Art Changed the World
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines the history of the art movement, bringing a fresh eye to the work of superstars Andy Warhol and Roy Lichtenstein and tracking down pioneers including James Rosenquist, Claes Oldenburg, Ed Ruscha, Peter Blake and Allen Jones. He also explores pop art's fascination with celebrity, advertising and the mass media, and travels to China to discover how a new generation of artists is reinventing it for the 21st century(n)

programme.title: A Day in the Life of Andy Warhol
programme.desc: Stephen Smith presents a profile of the artist, who created some of the most celebrated art of the 20th century, and pieces together a typical day in his life during the mid-1960s, told through encounters with some of Warhol's closest friends and confidantes. With contributions from John Giorno, Brigid Berlin, Gerard Malanga, Bibbe Hanson and Jane Holzer(n)

programme.title: The Art Mysteries
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines Gauguin's Vision After the Sermon,an epic 17th-century religious painting exploring temptation and desire. He uncovers some unusual connections with sumo wrestling, Victor Hugo's Les Miserables, and a 17-year-old girl called Madeleine(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain: The Greatest Discoveries
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts re-examines key archaeological sites of prehistoric Britain(n)

programme.title: Britain Beneath Your Feet
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary in which Dallas Campbell takes a unique look at Britain, exploring the hidden wonders beneath people's feet. Along the way he finds out how the Shard of London stays standing on soft clay and reveals how Edinburgh was set upon an ancient volcano. The presenter also abseils down an underground waterfall higher than Niagara, canoes along a secret river under the city of Bristol and discovers a vast root system beneath one of the nation's oldest oak trees(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: Documentary about traditional Japanese craftspeople, beginning by following Shiro Kunimitsu and his family as they craft a katana of exceptional sharpness and durability, continuing a tradition begun by their ancestors nearly three centuries ago(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: A dramatised account of the key moments in the lives of Henry VIII's wives, combining drama with the presenter's own contemporary historical comment. In this episode, Lucy follows Catherine of Aragon through her emotional and physical struggles to give Henry the heir he so desperately needs to continue his dynasty. When Catherine finally gives birth to a baby girl, Henry's eye wanders to the ladies at court - first Mary Boleyn, and then to her sister, Anne(n)

programme.title: George Best: All By Himself (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the reserved young football prodigy from Belfast who became a global superstar, but whose career was blighted by his battle with the pressures of fame. The film reveals how the player dubbed El Beatle helped Manchester United to win two league titles and the European Cup, but his extravagant lifestyle led to various problems, most notably alcoholism(n)

programme.title: Van Meegeren: The Forger Who Fooled the Nazis
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon investigates the story of Dutch artist and forger Han van Meegeren, who made millions during the Second World War selling fake Vermeers in Nazi-occupied Holland. Following a trail of evidence across Europe, the art critic pieces together how Van Meegeren fooled the art establishment - and even swindled Hermann Goering, selling him what was then one of the most expensive paintings in the world(n)

programme.title: Sword, Musket & Machine Gun: Britain's Armed History
programme.desc: Dr Sam Willis charts the evolution of weaponry in Britain from 1800 to the First World War, looking at the drive to develop ever-more precise weapons, from artillery shells and rifles to the Maxim machine gun. Sam test-fires a Brown Bess musket, the infantry weapon of choice at Waterloo in 1815 and looks at efforts to make artillery more effective on the battlefield with the invention of ‘spherical case shot', a new type of shell that was named after its inventor - Henry Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Japanese contemporary artist Yayoi Kusama's work pushed boundaries that often alienated her from her peers and those in power in the art world. Heather Lenz's documentary profiles her work, including the much-visited Infinity Mirror Room installations, which defies classification(n)

programme.title: George Best: All By Himself (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the reserved young football prodigy from Belfast who became a global superstar, but whose career was blighted by his battle with the pressures of fame. The film reveals how the player dubbed El Beatle helped Manchester United to win two league titles and the European Cup, but his extravagant lifestyle led to various problems, most notably alcoholism(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the ingenious ways in which ants have collaborated to colonise the planet like no other animal. Natural air conditioning systems keep them cool in their nests, shelters made from their own bodies protect them from the elements, and a strong sense of smell helps them to overpower animals hundreds of times larger than themselves. Plus, a look at recent research that reveals how ant colonies are capable of immunising themselves against certain diseases(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer and materials engineer Zoe Laughlin dismantles and dissects three classic items to understand the wonders of form, function and material that go into making them, before building her own truly bespoke versions step by step. She begins with modern classic the training shoe, meeting some of the UK's leading designers and manufacturers, as well as young inventors working on sustainable creations such as material made by bacteria and self-deodorising fabrics(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic enters a deadly world of jet-setting espionage, double agents and sinister conspiracies as he decodes that most thrilling of genres - the spy movie(n)

programme.title: Pinewood: 80 Years of Movie Magic
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross gains unprecedented access to the iconic British film studio, meeting Joan Collins and Barbara Windsor to discuss their experiences on set. The site in Buckinghamshire was transformed into a studio during the 1930s and became home to the James Bond, Harry Potter, Superman and Carry On film series, and Ross goes behind the scenes to tackle some daring and spectacular stunts of his own(n)

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks
programme.desc: Hollywood composers including Hans Zimmer, James Cameron, Danny Elfman, John Williams, Quincy Jones and Trent Reznor combine to give a privileged look inside the musical challenges and creative secrecy of the film score(n)

programme.title: In My Skin
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the life of 16-year-old Bethan Gwyndaf as she deals with the painfully real anxieties and insecurities of teenage life, and navigates her feelings about her unconventional family. Bethan desperately tries to keep the truth of her home life a secret from her friends, but with her mother Katrina sectioned in a mental facility near her school, and her father Dilwyn is a Hell's Angel who drives a rag-and-bone truck, flying under the radar is not so easy for her. programme.desc: Danny and Steve 'welcome' new boss Ted Case into the UCOS office in Sasha's absence, but for all his experience, keen eye for detail and a killer instinct for detecting liars, they aren't as sold on his superstitious quirks. programme.title: Inside Autism: A Tiny Bit of Magic
programme.desc: Children with autism spend a week on an intensive therapy camp, where specialist therapists try to transform challenging behaviours in just one week. programme.title: In My Skin
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the life of 16-year-old Bethan Gwyndaf as she deals with the painfully real anxieties and insecurities of teenage life, and navigates her feelings about her unconventional family. Bethan desperately tries to keep the truth of her home life a secret from her friends, but with her mother Katrina sectioned in a mental facility near her school, and her father Dilwyn is a Hell's Angel who drives a rag-and-bone truck, flying under the radar is not so easy for her. Starring Gabrielle Creevy, Jo Hartley and Rhodri Meilir(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Having escaped from one bleak situation to end up in another, things are not looking good for Villanelle. Meanwhile, Eve puts her obsession with her own work to one side and goes drinking with Niko to get to know his colleagues. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Villanelle and Konstantin take a trip to Amsterdam, where Villanelle completes a job in spectacular style - even by her standards. With the operation seemingly in disarray, Carolyn is called in to face the music with her boss. However, after the discovery of another dead body, Eve and her team make a breakthrough in the case. Zoe Wanamaker guest stars(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: HealthCheck UK Live
programme.desc: Xand Van Tulleken and Michelle Ackerley find out why this is the best time to give up smoking and ask whether vaping is really any safer than cigarettes. Angela Rippon puts on her gardening gloves for a challenge that is both intellectual and physical(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell explores a cottage in Saffron Walden, Essex, Dion Dublin visits a terrace in Manchester, and Martin Roberts looks at a plot of land near Wolverhampton(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A claim for a solitaire diamond is not as clear cut as the owner would have insurers believe and investigators deal with a rear-end shunt that has plenty of twists and turns(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams find items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp also visits the Stamford Mercury News Archive(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Things are difficult for Ruhma and Doug when Carrie is taken to hospital. Rob and PC Dajani try to work out who is telling the truth between two sisters in a complicated arson case(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: The shooting of two men links a slick casino owner to a suffering woman and her rash son. DI Bradford gets some bad news. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small and Bailey Patrick(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a drop-arm sofa, an old chest and a train set from Witley Recycling Centre, enlisting the help of restorer Peter Fournel and upcycler Jacqui Joseph(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel around Yorkshire, finding such rarities as a vintage set of weights and a Victorian skirt lifter, before an auction in Rotherham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Alex Scott host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, where the panel discuss the coronavirus and the implications that are affecting the UK and the rest of the world(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: The McGregors receives devastating news that rocks their family, while Kamal is inaugurated as prime minister and tightens racial laws across Albion - all the while battling to keep his personal secrets in the past. Sephy desperately tries to get through to Callum, but he is being pulled further and further into the Liberation Militia. Jude wants his brother to listen to Dorn - he is the one who is making a difference. As Callum takes matters into his own hands, Meggie introduces the Hadleys to someone who makes Sephy question everything she has ever known(n)

programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: The Lang Hame
programme.desc: Part two of two. Actor and writer Dan Gordon explores Ulster's graveyards, uncovering each county's stories of war, revolution, industry and culture from an Ulster-Scots perspective, and discovering how broader strands of social history are interwoven with the lives and deaths of the ordinary and extraordinary people buried in them(n)

programme.desc: Gregor MacLeod and his trusted bakers and cooks reveal the recipes they turn to when on a strict budget. Gregor visits his neighbour in Lewis who shows him a great way to use up mashed potato by making sweet and chocolatey macaroons with it. He then heads to Kyle of Lochalsh, where an accomplished cook shows him how she uses tinned salmon to make a delicious tart. Back at home, Gregor puts old bananas to good use in his sticky caramel upside-down cake(n)

programme.desc: The story of the Campbell family from the Isle of Barra. Tony Kearney is a well-known Scottish actor and broadcaster, but his family's talents extend back generations. programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Six-part series following Kate, a working-class woman who runs an old-fashioned cafe in a seaside town, as she develops a strong, if sometimes volatile, friendship with an asylum-seeking African doctor, Koji. With Kate and Koji's undercover clinic in full swing, the last thing they need is a visit from Dr Radwan. Medium keeps Koji calm, and Kate cooks up a surefire way to get back at an adversary. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola, Blake Harrison, Barbara Flynn and Victor McGuire(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie is horrified that her comments about Roxanna have been interpreted by her Polish father Emil as racist, and she is relieved when they understand what she actually did say. Peter has to contend with a campaign for his resignation, as journalists try to dig up his family skeletons. Meanwhile, money lenders Patrick and Phil put the pressure on Alex to repay his debts, and Marcus and Jill's relationship blossoms when he accompanies her to the planning meeting at the village hall for the village celebrations(n)

programme.title: Culinary Genius
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tests the contestants' knife skills, tasking them to slice savoy cabbage. He then gives them 10 minutes to extract a whole lobster from its shell using a variety of implements and immaculately present it whole. The final task is to create an impressive lobster dish. Once the challenges are over, the three best finalists press on for the coveted title and cash prize. Presented by Fern Britton(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Despite Kate being sued after an accident in the cafe, she refuses to back down and Koji is reluctant to get involved. Although Medium is convinced she should just apologise and get it over with, Kate suspects the legal threats may have something to do with Councillor Bone. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. In the first episode, Paul meets seven staffie puppies whose mother has rejected them, before he visits a 13-year-old mongrel who was found wandering the streets of North London. Elsewhere, Paul lends a hand building a ladder and a platform for nine-year-old Archie(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie is in need of some time alone so she heads to the pub to see if she can get a room for the night, where she learns the real story of landlord Brian. When she finds out there are no rooms free for her to stay, Maggie decides to go and stay with daughter-in-law Becka, and the pair have a rare moment of bonding. Meanwhile, Peter is working late at the school when his secretary Karen reveals her feelings towards him in a very unexpected way. Later, Alex desperately tries to find the 'lottery' winnings' to pay off the lenders(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Robbie Lyle - Football Fans Under Their Skin
programme.desc: Arsenal fan Robbie Lyle looks at racism directed at football supporters, revealing how he has been abused and meeting others speaking out for the first time about their experiences of racism in football - both in the past and today(n)

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: Ellie and Hardy bring a new suspect in for questioning and in a search of this suspect's premises, Ellie finds devastating new evidence linking him to the crime. Back at the station, he gives them his detailed version of events, but when they dig deeper they begin to doubt his story, while DC Katie Harford confronts Ellie and Hardy with an uncomfortable truth about her role in the investigation. The fact that Ian has taken a personal item of Trish's without her knowledge comes to light(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: How seriously could the coronavirus pandemic damage President's Trump's chances of re-election later this year? As the United States grapples with a full-blown health crisis, ITV's Washington Correspondent Robert Moore asks whether his controversial leadership style has properly addressed or even exacerbated the challenge ahead(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: The financial journalist presents money-saving tips to help consumers cope with the current economic climate, offering expert advice on everything from premium bonds, student loans and tax credits to supermarket loyalty schemes and high-street shopping(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: An insight into the world of the driving test, filmed over a two-year period and offering unprecedented access to the Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA), with cameras fitted inside and outside of the test car. In the first episode, examiner Wayne has the task of taking 27-year-old Shaunna from Hull through the driving test, which will be her 12th attempt at passing. In Cardiff, Drew is desperate to pass, if only to stop the arguments with his partner Karl, who's been teaching him to drive. Narrated by Alan Carr(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Having previously toured their own countries, Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix now hit the highways of of the States - although in the first episode, they are in Mexico, on a mission to taste the world's best breakfast. Along the way, the trio take part in a chili-eating competition, have a beach buggy race and watch some traditional Mexican wrestling - but it isn't long before they are persuaded not take part(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Security Officer Estefania investigates an oddly shaped object, and Passenger Experience Manager Harry deals with a medical emergency. Elsewhere, Border Force Bob helps a circus performer with visa troubles(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Andrew is pushed to the edge and as he runs out of options, he concocts a plan to take drastic measures. But first, he will need help. In the present day, a vital piece of evidence comes to light and Laura seeks to discover its origin. Now in a desperate position, she must use all her wits to get the information she needs, but with Katy and Liam's assistance, she is led to a startling new suspect. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Zoe Tapper, Richie Campbell and Katherine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Mateo's undocumented status causes trouble once again, while Glenn and Amy do battle with an online troll. Jonah and Cheyenne provide legal counsel for Mateo - though it could prove unhelpful. Comedy, starring America Ferrera(n)

programme.desc: Steph from Manchester picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the menus they have created. When the three dinners have been eaten, she decides who she wants to see again. Narrated by Charlotte Hudson(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: A rower from Nottingham, a barman from Luton, a lifeguard from London and a football fan from Ipswich enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness presides on the matching process, and also catches up on the latest dating action from all the couples in Fernando's as the dating game bows out for another series(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates viewers' videos featuring celebrity lookalikes. Holly Willoughby's husband risks everything to catch a glimpse of her in the nude, while Gregg Wallace tries to outsmart his ex-wife to retrieve his CD collection. Plus, what the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge get up to when the Queen isn't looking(n)

programme.title: Yes Man (2008)
programme.desc: A depressed and anti-social loans adviser attends a self-improvement course and decides to start saying yes to every opportunity that comes his way. His outlook is immediately transformed, but he soon realises that some invitations should not be accepted and struggles to find a balance in his life. Comedy, loosely based on the autobiographical book by Danny Wallace, starring Jim Carrey, Zooey Deschanel, Bradley Cooper and John Michael Higgins(n)

programme.desc: Fifty years ago in Caernarfon at his investiture, Prince Charles embarked on a unique relationship with a country that has shaped his life and work. programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Hard-up local employer Albert Ross is suspected of stealing money from a blind man - and as events unfold, it begins to seem increasingly likely he is also putting profits before the welfare of his disgruntled workforce. Meanwhile, David risks his life to save dispirited musician Keith Drake from a watery grave, and inspires him to set about rebuilding his career(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin tells Don about his fling. Reg is horrified to hear the Americans are coming to look over Firmans. Tracy is rushed to hospital after taking drugs(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf and Audrey struggle having all the children living at their house. Ken waits anxiously by Tracy's hospital bedside. Deirdre arrives to see Tracy(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty encourages Britt to make peace with her father. Eric approaches the Dingles about getting some cheap alcohol for them for the wedding. Kathy moves into her new flat(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles receive a letter from Sam in prison. Annie has a shock in store for Amos. Luke is worried what his family will say about the grandfather clock being sold(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Satanic symbols painted on the church door convince the Reverend John Peggotty there is an evil presence in the churchyard, but George is certain it is the work of hooligans. However, an attack on Lord Ashfordly's godson Chas uncovers information that suggests a more personal motive, leading the investigation into the world of drugs and rock music. Elsewhere, Gina frets over some personal news. Guest starring Jeff Rawle, Adam Croasdell and Adam Kotz(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When the trio decide they need a fourth person to share the flat, Robin is determined it will be a woman. Seventies comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Ruth talks her cantankerous landlord Rigsby into renting his newly vacated room to a friend of hers. Pilot episode of the classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: After Joe's uniform goes missing and the police station is broken into, Geoff admits his Panda car was stolen the day before. George suspects an old adversary of deliberately stirring up trouble. Elsewhere, Rachel reassures a witness in a high profile case he is safe under her care, but a neighbour becomes suspicious of her visits(n)

programme.desc: Annie is less than pleased with Amos's response to her proposal. Luke attempts to buy the clock back from Eric. Tina finds out Luke hasn't told his parents about the wedding(n)

programme.desc: Reg is worried for his and Curly's jobs. Ken and Deirdre unite over their concern for Tracy. Vicky considers investing in Steve's deal(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: After Joe's uniform goes missing and the police station is broken into, Geoff admits his Panda car was stolen the day before. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Charles: 50 Years a Prince
programme.desc: Fifty years ago in Caernarfon at his investiture, Prince Charles embarked on a unique relationship with a country that has shaped his life and work. While acknowledging a sometimes troubled and turbulent history, this documentary celebrates Wales through the eyes of a prince who has made the role his own. Narrated by Luke Evans(n)

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet voices her concerns to Dodson about Gill's drinking, hoping it can be dealt with amicably, so she's unnerved when her observations are taken more seriously than she intended. programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect Cowley's former girlfriend, now a controversial evangelist, from an assassin, but are unaware that the would-be hitman is right under their nose. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An Israeli minister is abducted, and the ensuing suburban siege triggers an airborne pursuit as Bodie and Doyle try to save the day. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: James Bond travels to the Bahamas on the trail of two nuclear warheads stolen from a British aircraft. programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer heads off round the Caribbean in mega cruise ship MSC Divina. She and her fellow passengers stop off in Nassau in the Bahamas, where Jane finds solace in the state's oldest Baptist church, and St Martin, where she seeks an intimate underwater audience with a host of sharks. Back on board, Jane is startled by the scale of everything - from the food and activities to the sheer number of people(n)

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow presents the life and work of the Western world's most gifted artists, beginning with Giotto di Bondone, the son of a Tuscan shepherd. Born in 1267, Giotto began his apprenticeship at the age of 12, and with his naturalistic treatment of medieval Christian iconography, was recognised as the first in a line of great painters and architects who contributed to the Italian Renaissance(n)

programme.title: Wildlife SOS

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
